description: Something recently made, gathered, or arrived is 'newai' while it keeps the condition expected of it. Fresh fruit has not yet spoiled; fresh air has newly entered and is pleasant to breathe. 'newu' (new) marks recent introduction or possession, while 'wolu' (healthy) concerns a living system's condition rather than recency.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n' and carry the voice through the nose into true-mid front 'e'. Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then open into stressed central 'a'. Raise the front of the tongue for a separate high-front unrounded 'i' syllable. Keep the 'a.i' hiatus clear.
sound_symbolism: The shared 'ne.w' opening keeps 'newai' close to 'newu' (new). Its final 'a.i' hiatus moves sharply from open central 'a' to high-front 'i'; the quick brightening suggests something newly uncovered.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Before powered refrigeration, people needed daily practical judgement about whether food and water were still 'newai'.

semantic_domains.physical: retention of an expected recent quality before spoilage or fading
semantic_domains.temporal: recent making, gathering, arrival, or exposure
